description: After a mistake or harm, 'soa' says 'sorry'. It expresses regret and can open an apology, but the words and actions that follow must do the repair. 'hao' (pardon) asks for repetition; despite the overlap in English, it is not an apology.
articulatory_notes: Hold the tongue blade near the alveolar ridge and send unvoiced air through its central groove for 's', then draw the tongue back and round the lips for mid-back 'o'. Release the rounding and lower the jaw into open central 'a' for the separate second syllable. Stress 'so'.
usage_notes: For a small bump, 'soa' may be the whole exchange. When harm is larger, it can begin a fuller acknowledgement and repair.
